name: Kobold
description: Kobolds are household spirits in Germanic folklore, often mischievous but generally helpful. They are believed to inhabit human dwellings, mines, and sometimes ships. Their appearance varies widely, from small, wizened humanoids to animalistic forms. Kobolds are known for their ability to shapeshift and their unpredictable nature. They can be helpful, performing chores and protecting the home, but they can also be troublesome, playing pranks and causing minor disruptions if they are not treated with respect. Offerings of food and drink are often left out to appease them. In mines, they are believed to be responsible for both finding veins of ore and leading miners astray. Their presence is a sign of good or bad luck, depending on their mood and how they are treated. They are deeply connected to the home and the family that resides there, often staying for generations. Their relationship with humans is based on mutual respect and understanding, though misunderstandings can lead to mischief and misfortune. They are considered a part of the household, and their well-being is linked to the prosperity of the family. Disrespecting a Kobold can bring about misfortune and even disaster.
subtitle: Mischievous, helpful household spirit.
appearance: Kobolds are often described as small, wizened humanoids with wrinkled faces. Their size can vary, sometimes described as being no larger than a child, while other accounts depict them as being closer to the size of a dwarf. They can appear as old men with beards or as animalistic creatures, such as cats or dogs. Some descriptions mention them wearing small, tattered clothing, while others say they are naked. The key feature is their unpredictable appearance and ability to change form.
habitat: Kobolds primarily inhabit human dwellings, including houses, barns, and even ships. In mines, they are believed to live deep underground, guarding veins of ore or leading miners to their doom. They are most often found in older buildings and places where they have established a long-term connection.
behavior: Kobolds are known for their mischievous and unpredictable behavior. They can be helpful, performing chores such as cleaning and repairing things, but they can also be troublesome, playing pranks and causing minor disruptions. Their behavior often depends on how they are treated; if they are respected and given offerings, they are more likely to be helpful. If they are ignored or disrespected, they can become vengeful and cause misfortune.

keyMyths.title: The Milk Thief
keyMyths.description: A Kobold was angered when a farmer stopped leaving out milk. In retaliation, it soured all of the milk in the barn, causing great hardship for the farmer and his family.

keyMyths.title: The Mine Kobold
keyMyths.description: Miners who disrespected the mine Kobold found themselves lost in the tunnels or injured by falling rocks. Those who left offerings of food and drink were led to rich veins of ore.

sources.author: Jacob Grimm & Wilhelm Grimm
sources.description: A collection of German folktales, featuring several stories about Kobolds.
sources.text: A collection of German folktales, featuring several stories about Kobolds.

sources.author: Katharine Briggs
